metadata.purpose: Gather public consultant reports (McKinsey, Wood Mackenzie, Rystad Energy) and related sources on NCS crude quality, reservoir characteristics, and decline rates for the decline_quality analysis.
search_queries_run: "Wood Mackenzie" Norway NCS oil reservoir API gravity field characteristics
"Rystad Energy" NCS Norway field development reservoir characteristics oil quality
Norway oil production crude quality API gravity report 2024 2025
McKinsey Norway oil gas production decline NCS report
Johan Sverdrup Ekofisk Troll API gravity GOR reservoir properties
"Rystad Energy" Johan Sverdrup decline rate field characteristics
Grane Heidrun Draugen NCS heavy oil API gravity reservoir properties
"Wood Mackenzie" North Sea crude quality grades Brent Forties Oseberg
NCS oil field formation Jurassic Cretaceous chalk sandstone reservoir characteristics
Equinor Edvard Grieg Ivar Aasen Martin Linge reservoir characteristics API gravity
Aker BP Valhall Skarv Alvheim crude oil API gravity GOR reservoir
Norwegian crude assay Snorre Statfjord Gullfaks Asgard API gravity sulfur
"Rystad Energy" NCS resource report 2024 2025 production outlook field

reports_identified.title: Norway upstream summary Report
reports_identified.scope: Norway covered as Europe's largest oil and gas producer, with high development activity across the shelf. Detailed field-level data not accessible publicly.

wood_mackenzie.public_data_extracted: No reservoir-level API/GOR/formation data accessible without subscription. Wood Mackenzie's NCS work is referenced in third-party media coverage but specific field parameters are behind paywall.

reports_identified.title: Cooldown on the horizon after NCS spending surge
key_findings: Offshore NCS spending peaks NOK 262 bn in 2025; declines to NOK 160 bn in 2030 (-39%).
29 projects sanctioned under temporary tax regime (Jun 2020–end 2022) including Aker BP Yggdrasil hub and Valhall PWP-Fenris.
Most NCS oil output still comes from giants sanctioned under standard tax regime (esp. Johan Sverdrup, Phase 2 online Dec 2022).
Exploration capex rises from NOK 29 bn (2024) to NOK 37 bn (2025).

reports_identified.title: Technologies to improve NCS competitiveness (OG21/Rystad Energy)
reports_identified.scope: NCS competitiveness, technology, recovery factors. May contain field-level reservoir data but not extracted by WebFetch (PDF binary).

reports_identified.title: Norway has lowest upstream CO2 emission intensity (Rystad)
key_findings: Johan Sverdrup emits <5% of world-average upstream CO2/bbl.

johan_sverdrup.decline_rate_2026_forecast: 10-20% YoY decline expected in 2026 (Equinor guidance, Rystad referenced).
johan_sverdrup.secondary_recovery: Water and gas injection support high offtake rates and plateau beyond 2025 conditional on Phase 3.

reports_identified.title: McKinsey on North Sea cost competitiveness (referenced in Carnegie Endowment piece, 2016)
key_findings: North Sea was, ~15 yrs ago, on par with the most economic oil-producing regions globally.
As province entered long-term decline, costs grew faster than in any other oil & gas region in the world.
Cost inefficiencies identified; McKinsey recommended increased operator cooperation as mitigation.
UKCS peaked at 2.9 Mb/d (1999); NCS peaked at 3.4 Mb/d (2001).

mckinsey.public_data_extracted: McKinsey's NCS-specific work focuses on cost and decline economics; no public reservoir-level API/GOR data found in this search.

field_level_reservoir_data.note: Aggregated from public sources surfaced during consultant searches; not all data is from consultancies directly.

fields.permeability_darcy: 0.5 to 40 (median core perm range across wells)
fields.reservoir_quality_notes: Multi-darcy, exceptional reservoir quality; supports plateau via water + gas injection.
fields.decline_outlook: 10-20% YoY decline expected 2026 (Equinor); revised 13% YoY full year 2026 average.

fields.permeability_notes: Primary perm low due to constricted pore connections; fracture network materially improves productivity.
fields.benchmark_status: Part of BFOET / Dated Brent basket
Part of BFOET / Dated Brent basket

fields.api_gravity_note: Slightly denser than Brent; structural discount vs. Brent/Oseberg.

fields.benchmark_status: Part of BFOET / Dated Brent basket

fields.formation: Poorly consolidated turbidite sandstone at ~5,500 ft TVD
fields.blend_post_2015: Grane blend now commingled with Edvard Grieg and Ivar Aasen volumes, lifting blend API to ~31.5 (medium).
sources: Oil & Gas Journal CO2 injection study

fields.notes: Volumes routed into Grane blend, lifting blend quality from heavy to medium.

fields.formation: Middle Jurassic Hugin Formation shallow marine sandstone

fields.classification: very light (lighter than Dated Brent basket)
fields.location: Northern Norwegian Sea, water depth 350-450 m

fields.issue_2024: Production below 2P estimate due to higher GOR in newer wells (gas breakthrough/coning).
fields.vilje_satellite: Sand reservoir with very good properties; undersaturated oil.

fields.field: Statfjord Blend (Statfjord + Snorre + Sygna + Statfjord N/E)
fields.classification: light, low-sulfur (similar to Brent Blend, slightly lower S, less atmospheric residue).
sources: Oil & Gas Journal 'Guide to World Crudes'

fields.refining_value: Well-suited for gasoline/diesel/jet plus LPG and naphtha petrochem feed.

fields.ranking: Among Norway's six largest fields by total resources.

ncs_reservoir_geology_summary.source: Search synthesis (Ekofisk industriminne; ScienceDirect 'The habitat of North Sea oil'; Norskpetroleum)

main_play_types.type: Upper Cretaceous chalk on salt domes
Jurassic sandstones on crests of faulted horsts / tilted blocks

decline_and_quality_macro_findings.outlook: Sodir 2024 Resource Report scenarios all show decline; pace depends on exploration and technology.
decline_and_quality_macro_findings.crude_quality_trend: Johan Sverdrup (medium-sour, 28.7 API, 0.8% S) increasingly dominates NCS output, pulling Norwegian export basket toward heavier/more-sour grades vs. legacy light sweet Brent/Ekofisk/Oseberg/Troll. This crude-quality shift is well-documented by S&P Global and Hellenic Shipping News.

gaps_and_limitations: Wood Mackenzie and Rystad Energy field-level NCS databases (Asset Service, OSR Norway) are paywalled; only summary insights and media references are publicly accessible.
OG21/Rystad PDF on NCS competitiveness was retrieved but returned as binary; not parsed in this search.

Public APIs for Heidrun, Draugen, Martin Linge, Yggdrasil API/GOR data not surfaced in this batch of searches - recommend operator annual reports + ExxonMobil/TotalEnergies assay libraries + Sodir factpages as next step.
No quantitative McKinsey field-level reservoir data exists publicly; their NCS work is cost/efficiency oriented.

Olaussen et al. - Johan Sverdrup reservoir characterization
Oil & Gas Journal - Important Norwegian crude assays updated
Oil & Gas Journal - CO2 injection studied for Norwegian field (Grane)
Carnegie Endowment - North Sea future (McKinsey reference)
